mnemonics.title: Degree Definitions
mnemonics.content: I-P-N: Isolated = 0, Pendant = 1, Null = all 0s - Think 'I Have Zero' for Isolated, 'One for Lonely Pendant' for degree 1

mnemonics.title: Tree Pendant Rule
mnemonics.content: TREE has 2 LEAFs - Remember: Every tree with n≥2 has at least 2 leaf (pendant) vertices - Like a tree in nature has leaves at the tips

acronyms.usage: Vertex with degree 1, also called leaf vertex
Vertex with degree 0, no incident edges
Graph with no edges, all vertices isolated
Edge incident to a pendant/leaf vertex

visualTips.visualization: Draw a vertex floating alone with no lines connecting to anything - like a solitary island
Draw a vertex at the end of a single line - like a leaf hanging from a single stem
Draw multiple vertices scattered with absolutely no lines between any of them
Count lines (edges) coming out of each vertex - draw small numbers next to vertices showing their degree

analogies.analogy: Like a house with no driveway or road connecting to it - completely cut off from the neighborhood
Like a single leaf hanging from a tree branch by its stem - one connection point
Like houses in different cities with no roads between them - each is isolated
A rope with only one end tied to something - if you cut it, the rope falls away
